Start Your Project
Open for focused work

I build websites, web apps, and software systems that stay clear under pressure.

I take on products that need more than a clean surface. The interface has to make sense. The state underneath it has to stay correct. I build both with care, from public sites to internal tools to systems where trust breaks fast when the data goes stale or the logic slips.

See my work Start Your Project
How the work starts

App builds that stay fast, legible, and easy to trust.

I start with the parts that fail first. State. Handoffs. Load paths. Slow screens. If the product feels calm, it is because I did that work before I polished the UI.

Scope before screens.

I map the states, inputs, and handoffs first so the build solves the actual operating problem.

Working software early.

I put the useful path in front of you early so feedback lands on real behavior, not guesses.

Performance with intent.

I watch response time, rendering cost, and state churn so the product holds up on real devices once people start using it.

See how the work runs
Websites and product apps

I build public-facing products that explain themselves fast and still hold up once the logic gets deeper.

Internal tools and system work

I build operational software for teams that need clear state, fewer manual gaps, and less guesswork.

Trading systems and fintech

I take on builds where reconciliation, execution state, contributions, payouts, or other trust-heavy flows need more care.

Product reliability and ownership

I harden the logic, interface, and handoff path so the product stays readable after launch.

Selected work

Two builds with real engineering weight.

One system keeps several runtimes in sync. The other keeps community finance legible for the people who rely on it.

Trading infrastructure

SMC SuperFIB

A cross-runtime trading system that keeps indicator logic, API state, execution clients, and operator visibility aligned.

I had to keep multiple runtimes in agreement without forcing the operator to read raw logs to understand the system state.

Read the SuperFIB case study
Community finance

Stokvel Society

A fintech product for contributions, ledger visibility, and payout flows that members can follow without confusion.

I turned a trust-heavy community finance workflow into software that keeps balances, history, and payouts easy to read.

Read the Stokvel Society case study
Next step

Read the work, then decide if the fit is there.

The case studies show how I scope risk, design state, and carry a build through launch. You can judge the fit from the work itself.
